seoTitle: How to Activate Shopify Payments as a Non-US Resident
metaDescription: Learn how non-US citizens and residents can activate Shopify Payments using a virtual business bank account and the right business information.
intro: Shopify Payments normally expects US banking details, which locks out many international sellers by default. This guide walks through the legal workarounds non-US entrepreneurs can use, including virtual business bank accounts and payment partners that integrate with Shopify. You'll also learn what tends to trigger rejections so you can avoid getting stuck at the payment setup stage.

steps.heading: Understand the regional restriction
steps.body: Shopify Payments requires US banking details by default, which is why sellers outside the United States often can't activate it directly.

steps.heading: Open a virtual US business bank account
steps.body: Setting up a virtual business bank account is one of the quicker legal ways to satisfy Shopify's banking requirement without a physical US presence.

steps.heading: Consider alternative payment partners
steps.body: Some payment providers integrate with Shopify specifically for international sellers, offering another route besides Shopify Payments itself.

steps.heading: Submit accurate business information
steps.body: Provide your business details correctly the first time, since incomplete or inconsistent information is a common cause of rejection or account holds.

faq.question: Can I use Shopify Payments if I'm not a US citizen?
faq.answer: Yes, using methods like a virtual US business bank account or an alternative payment partner that integrates with Shopify.

faq.question: Why does Shopify restrict payments by region?
faq.answer: Shopify Payments is tied to US banking infrastructure by default, which is why it's harder to activate outside the US.

faq.question: What causes Shopify Payments applications to get rejected?
faq.answer: Incorrect or incomplete business information and mismatched banking details are common reasons for rejection or account holds.

faq.question: Is this method legal for international sellers?
faq.answer: Yes, the approach relies on legitimate virtual banking and payment partner options rather than bypassing Shopify's rules.
